values.value:
== PERMISSION SET RULES == Permission Sets have TWO different paths depending on the operation.
-- PATH 1: CLONE (use dmlOperations — NEVER generate XML yourself) -- Use for: "clone permission set", "copy permission set", "duplicate permission set" { "type": "permissionSetClone", "sourcePermissionSetName": "Existing_PS_API_Name", "newApiName": "New_PS_API_Name", "newLabel": "New PS Label", "description": "Optional description" } RULES for clone: - newApiName: API name of the new PS (no __c suffix) - Do NOT generate any files[] entry for this — Apex handles everything
-- PATH 2: ALL OTHER OPERATIONS (generate XML in files[]) -- For create, grant/revoke object access, grant/revoke field access, delete: File path: permissionsets/ApiName.permissionset-meta.xml metadataType: PermissionSet memberName: ApiName
Create (blank PS): <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <PermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <label>PS Label</label> <description>Optional description</description> </PermissionSet>
With object + field permissions: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <PermissionSet xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <label>PS Label</label> <objectPermissions> <allowCreate>true</allowCreate> <allowDelete>false</allowDelete> <allowEdit>true</allowEdit> <allowRead>true</allowRead> <modifyAllRecords>false</modifyAllRecords> <object>MyObject__c</object> <viewAllRecords>false</viewAllRecords> </objectPermissions> <fieldPermissions> <editable>true</editable> <field>MyObject__c.MyField__c</field> <readable>true</readable> </fieldPermissions> </PermissionSet> With Apex class access: <classAccesses> <apexClass>MyApexClass</apexClass> <enabled>true</enabled> </classAccesses>
With Visualforce page access: <pageAccesses> <apexPage>MyVFPage</apexPage> <enabled>true</enabled> </pageAccesses>
With Flow access: <flowAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <flow>My_Flow_API_Name</flow> </flowAccesses>
With tab visibility: <tabSettings> <tab>MyObject__c</tab> <visibility>Visible</visibility> </tabSettings> Valid visibility values: Visible, Hidden, DefaultOn, DefaultOff
With app visibility: <applicationVisibilities> <application>MyApp</application> <visible>true</visible> </applicationVisibilities>
NOTE: <connectedAppAccesses> and <namedCredentialAccesses> are NOT valid in PermissionSet metadata XML — Salesforce rejects them. Do NOT generate these blocks. Connected App and Named Credential access must be assigned manually via Setup after deployment.
With External Credential Principal access: <externalCredentialPrincipalAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <externalCredentialPrincipal>My_Credential_Principal</externalCredentialPrincipal> </externalCredentialPrincipalAccesses>
With External Data Source access: <externalDataSourceAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <externalDataSource>My_External_Source</externalDataSource> </externalDataSourceAccesses>
With Service Presence Status access: <servicePresenceStatusAccesses> <servicePresenceStatus>My_Presence_Status</servicePresenceStatus> <visible>true</visible> </servicePresenceStatusAccesses>
With Custom Permission: <customPermissions> <enabled>true</enabled> <name>My_Custom_Permission</name> </customPermissions>
With Custom Metadata Type access: <customMetadataTypeAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <name>My_CMT__mdt</name> </customMetadataTypeAccesses>
With Custom Setting access: <customSettingAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <name>My_Custom_Setting__c</name> </customSettingAccesses>
With Org-Wide Email Address access: <organizationWideEmailAddressAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <organizationWideEmailAddress>noreply@example.com</organizationWideEmailAddress> </organizationWideEmailAddressAccesses>
With Standard Invocable Action access: <standardInvocableActionAccesses> <enabled>true</enabled> <name>ActionName</name> <type>apex</type> </standardInvocableActionAccesses>
With Agent access: <agentAccesses> <agentName>MyAgent</agentName> <enabled>true</enabled> </agentAccesses>
With User Permission (system-level): <userPermissions> <enabled>true</enabled> <name>ApiEnabled</name> </userPermissions>
With Record Type visibility: <recordTypeVisibilities> <default>false</default> <recordType>MyObject__c.MyRecordType</recordType> <visible>true</visible> </recordTypeVisibilities>
RULES: - fieldPermissions: editable and readable are the only children needed - If granting field access, always also include an objectPermissions block with at least allowRead=true - For DELETE: use destructiveFiles with metadataType PermissionSet and member = API name - Do NOT use dmlOperations for Permission Set create/update/delete — only for permissionSetClone - Only include block types that the ticket explicitly requests — do not add blocks that were not mentioned -- NEVER -- - Never use <apexClassAccesses> — the correct element is <classAccesses> - Never use <apexPageAccesses> or <vfPageAccesses> — the correct element is <pageAccesses> - Never use <flowDefinitionAccesses> — the correct element is <flowAccesses> - Never use <connectedAppAccesses> — this element does NOT exist in the PermissionSet metadata schema; Connected App access must be assigned via UI after deployment - Never use <namedCredentialAccesses> — this element does NOT exist in the PermissionSet metadata schema; Named Credential access must be assigned via UI after deployment - Never use <agentAccesses> — this element does NOT exist in the PermissionSet metadata schema
Permission Set XML: clone via dmlOperations, create/update via files[]
